title: The Smiley Face

jokes.text: Yeah, except she hasn't been here in three weeks. - Well, where is she? - She told me that her father was dying and she wanted to go home and spend some time with him and he hasn't died. He's kind of, you know, lingering.
jokes.setup: Assistant is missing for three weeks due to dying father
jokes.punchline: Father is just 'lingering' instead of dying, creating employment awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb setup - taking an uncomfortable social situation and making it about personal inconvenience. Dark but honest.

jokes.text: What if he hangs on for, like, six months? - That's the problem! - You're stuck. - I know, what can I do? I can't fire her.
jokes.setup: The predicament of the lingering father
jokes.punchline: Can't fire someone whose father is dying, even if he won't die
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the premise, realistic workplace dilemma made absurd.

jokes.text: You know, if he knew that his daughter's job was in jeopardy, based on hanging on, he'd die.
jokes.setup: The father won't die, threatening the daughter's job
jokes.punchline: Knowing about the job threat would motivate him to die
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant Larry logic - death as career consideration. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm Dino from Big Dog productions. I'm moving in next door. You can call me Dog. - Everybody calls me Dog.
jokes.setup: New neighbor introduces himself as Dino
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks to be called 'Dog' instead
jokes.explanation: Character establishment through absurd Hollywood affectation. Sets up future conflicts.

jokes.text: I can't believe you just gave away a cabinet. You never give up a cabinet. Never give up a cabinet.
jokes.setup: Larry casually agrees to share a kitchen cabinet
jokes.punchline: Jeff treats it like a cardinal sin with repeated emphasis
jokes.explanation: Establishes the cabinet as sacred real estate. Repetition creates comedy through obsession.

jokes.text: But you're shitting where you eat. - That's right. I'm shitting where I eat.
jokes.setup: Jeff warns Larry about dating the restaurant hostess
jokes.punchline: Larry embraces the crude metaphor with pride
jokes.explanation: Larry's defiant acceptance of bad advice. Character consistency in making poor decisions.

jokes.text: But when that happens... - And I will shit where I eat. - Or eat where you shat. - Or eat where I shat. - I've never seen it done.
jokes.setup: Larry plans to return to restaurant after inevitable breakup
jokes.punchline: Jeff flips the crude metaphor, Larry adopts it, Jeff admits fascination
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay escalation. Jeff's morbid curiosity about Larry's social experiment.

jokes.text: Oh, a smiley face. See, I hate that. I told her about the smiley faces. I can't stand it. And everybody uses them!
jokes.setup: Heidi sends Larry a text with a smiley face
jokes.punchline: Larry's disproportionate rage at innocent punctuation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pet peeve - minor social convention becomes major annoyance. Sets up episode theme.

jokes.text: What, are they gonna be in newspapers soon? 'New York times' headline: 'Unemployment drops :)'
jokes.setup: Larry's hatred of smiley faces
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to serious journalism using emoji
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation taking pet peeve to absurd conclusion. Great specific example.

jokes.text: Oh, man, you know, your cheek just touched mine on that hug. - Yeah, I hugged you. - I know, but still, check-to-cheek contact from men-- That's unacceptable.
jokes.setup: Normal friendly hug between men
jokes.punchline: Larry finds innocent contact unacceptable and says so
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness - making normal interactions uncomfortable through over-analysis.

jokes.text: Well, to be honest, things aren't so great with Susie and I right now. I think it'd be best if we'd bow out of Saturday night.
jokes.setup: Jeff declining dinner invitation with serious tone
jokes.punchline: Formal, rehearsed-sounding excuse that will be revealed as lie
jokes.explanation: Sets up the lie that will cause major problems later. Works better in context of full episode arc.

jokes.text: 'Things aren't so great right now...' With Susie and I, they're not so great. - '...With Susie and I.' - They're not great. Congratulations. That was gorgeous.
jokes.setup: Jeff's excuse to Stu
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff celebrate the successful lie like a performance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry and Jeff's complicity in social deception. Meta-theatrical.

jokes.text: By the way, things many times are not great with Susie and I. It's never stopped us from going to dinner.
jokes.setup: The excuse they just used about relationship troubles
jokes.punchline: Larry points out they regularly socialize despite constant marital problems
jokes.explanation: Good observation about the inconsistency of their excuse. Shows Larry's logical mind.

jokes.text: Be careful about the smiley faces. - What's wrong with the smiley face? - I feel like I'm getting a text from a 10-year-old.
jokes.setup: Larry addressing Heidi's texting habits
jokes.punchline: Compares adult woman to child over punctuation choice
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier complaint, showing Larry can't let petty grievances go.

jokes.text: Why can't you kiss and talk? I don't-- I don't know, I just feel like eating while you're talking-- - It's not mutually exclusive.
jokes.setup: Heidi wants to multitask romance and conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion over basic human intimacy logistics
jokes.explanation: Larry's social awkwardness extending to intimate moments. Treats romance like a logistical puzzle.

jokes.text: You know, I talk during intercourse too. - You do? - Yes. - I'm looking forward to that.
jokes.setup: Discussion about talking during intimate activities
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkwardly enthusiastic response to TMI
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from oversharing and Larry's awkward response.

jokes.text: Sorry. I didn't-- I didn't know. 'Cause I heard you guys talking. You can talk and make out. Well, not if it's good.
jokes.setup: Niece interrupts romantic moment, Larry says you can multitask
jokes.punchline: Young woman corrects Larry about quality intimacy
jokes.explanation: Young person schooling Larry about intimacy. Good callback with escalation.

jokes.text: That's really not your concern. - I've never heard of that. Okay, now you've heard of it.
jokes.setup: Mimi questions Larry's romantic theories
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact dismissal of her inexperience
jokes.explanation: Larry's condescending but matter-of-fact response to being questioned. Good economy.

jokes.text: Oh, do you have any cash? Just I can pay you back when I see you at the restaurant... How about 60 bucks?
jokes.setup: End of romantic evening, Heidi asks for money
jokes.punchline: Specific amount request after intimate dinner
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point. The casual money request after romance creates awkward dynamic.

jokes.text: What'd you do? You stuffed me into one cabinet! - Looks like it all fit in there. I gave you a cabinet. You took two?
jokes.setup: Larry discovers cabinet situation worsened
jokes.punchline: Dino's casual dismissal and admission of taking more
jokes.explanation: Escalation of cabinet conflict. Dino's casual taking advantage of Larry's niceness.

jokes.text: When they're done shooting the sharks, they will come back. You want to be fair about this thing? I'll take this cabinet, you take that cabinet and we'll split this one.
jokes.setup: Dino justifies taking more space
jokes.punchline: Offers 'fair' deal that gives him even more
jokes.explanation: Dino's transparent attempt to seem fair while taking more. Good character work.

jokes.text: Do they kind of huddle together and whisper? - No. Do they seem morose? Lugubrious?
jokes.setup: Larry asking about doctors' demeanor with dying patient
jokes.punchline: Using overly sophisticated vocabulary ('lugubrious') in casual conversation
jokes.explanation: Larry's pretentious vocabulary in inappropriate context. 'Lugubrious' is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: Larry, no! You never give up a cabinet. Never ever give up a cabinet.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Antoinette about cabinet situation
jokes.punchline: Same exact phrase as Jeff, showing universal cabinet wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing everyone knows this rule except Larry. Repetition for emphasis.

jokes.text: So sorry. No, I was at the dermatologist. And he kept me waiting for 45 minutes. - 45 minute-- Rivkin did?
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Richard's shock that their mutual doctor would do this
jokes.explanation: Sets up the lie that will spiral. Richard's reaction shows this is out of character for doctor.

jokes.text: Are you lying to me? - No! - Your voice just went up. - What? So what? - That means you're lying.
jokes.setup: Richard suspects Larry is lying about wait time
jokes.punchline: Voice pitch as lie detector technique
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about tell-tale signs of lying. Sets up voice pitch as recurring device.

jokes.text: The whole Watergate trial, They went 'no, I wasn't there!'
jokes.setup: Discussion about voice pitch indicating lies
jokes.punchline: Escalates to historical political scandal comparison
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from personal lie to historical scandal. Richard's neurotic overthinking.

jokes.text: So you still got the $60. - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Heidi didn't need to use the borrowed money
jokes.punchline: Awkward acknowledgment she still has Larry's money
jokes.explanation: Sets up the money conflict. Simple but effective awkwardness.

jokes.text: I shat where I ate all over Hollywood and Beverly Hills. - I'm down to four restaurants.
jokes.setup: Larry's pattern of dating restaurant staff
jokes.punchline: Quantified consequence - limited dining options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with escalation. Shows the real consequences of Larry's behavior pattern.

jokes.text: Mr. Chow's-- Out. The Palm-- Out. - Med Cafe-- Out.
jokes.setup: Larry listing his restaurant casualties
jokes.punchline: Specific high-end restaurants he can no longer visit
jokes.explanation: Great specificity showing real consequences. Economic listing builds impact.

jokes.text: I will be the Edmund Hillary... - The Ed-- - ...Of shitting where you eat. - You think so? - Yes yes. - The first guy?
jokes.setup: Larry's determination to return to the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Comparing dating mishaps to conquering Everest
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation comparing social faux pas to historic achievement. Perfect Larry grandiosity.

jokes.text: What's $60 by comparison to intercourse with a woman like that? - Still, it's a question of character.
jokes.setup: Richard dismisses the money issue
jokes.punchline: Larry's principled stance on small amount
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's stubborn principles vs Richard's pragmatism. Good character contrast.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Mr. Lincoln. - You're a fucking schmuck.
jokes.setup: Larry's principled stance on the money
jokes.punchline: Richard sarcastically compares him to Lincoln then insults him
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comparison followed by direct insult. Richard's exasperation with Larry's pettiness.

jokes.text: You get that cabinet back, there'll be a little something in it for you.
jokes.setup: Antoinette successfully organizing office
jokes.punchline: Larry promises reward for cabinet retrieval in suggestive way
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo about cabinet retrieval reward. Sets up Antoinette's motivation.

jokes.text: But, wait, I am dying to know why in the hell aren't Jeff and Susie coming? - What did they say? - They said that things aren't great right now.
jokes.setup: Miriam asking about absent dinner guests
jokes.punchline: Larry repeating the vague excuse verbatim
jokes.explanation: Sets up the chain reaction. Miriam's curiosity will cause problems.

jokes.text: Is he having an affair? - No! - Oooh, you heard it right? - Yeah. - That high-- - Heard what?
jokes.setup: Miriam's probing question about affair
jokes.punchline: Voice pitch analysis reveals Larry's lie
jokes.explanation: Callback to voice pitch device. Sets up the misunderstanding that will explode later.

jokes.text: My dad just died. My mom-- The last thing he said was 'where's Antoinette?' Guess where I was.
jokes.setup: Father's death and his final words
jokes.punchline: Antoinette was at Larry's office instead of deathbed
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant payoff to opening setup. Perfect ironic twist - she missed death for cabinet war.

jokes.text: Huh.
jokes.setup: Antoinette's tragic revelation about missing father's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inadequate response to tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - completely inappropriate underreaction to tragedy. Maximum comedy from minimum words.

jokes.text: As a matter of fact, Antoinette called me recently and asked me to play at his funeral. I'm playing 'Danny Boy' on the flute. - Is that so? - As they lower the casket.
jokes.setup: Doctor mentioning he knew the deceased
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic funeral performance details
jokes.explanation: Absurd details about funeral performance. Rivkin's self-important artistic contribution.

jokes.text: He recently canceled an appointment with me at the last minute and hasn't rescheduled yet. Have you any idea why such a thing would take place?
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about Richard's appointment behavior
jokes.punchline: Formal, overly elaborate way of asking simple question
jokes.explanation: Rivkin's pompous speech patterns. Sets up revelation that Larry's lie had consequences.

jokes.text: I have to masturbate before I come so I exhaust the area. - It's a whole to-do.
jokes.setup: Discussion about uncomfortable medical exam
jokes.punchline: Larry's TMI solution to medical anxiety
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Larry oversharing. Treating medical exam like logistical challenge.

jokes.text: These cabinets are mine-- Look! 'For Big Dog only. For Big Dog only.' - Big shit! You put the labels on, so what?
jokes.setup: Dino shows his cabinet labels
jokes.punchline: Larry dismisses the labels as meaningless power play
jokes.explanation: Escalation of cabinet conflict. Dino's territorial marking vs Larry's dismissal.

jokes.text: There are two things about Dog that everybody knows, okay? I am clean and I am fair.
jokes.setup: Dino defending his cabinet takeover
jokes.punchline: Claims fairness while clearly being unfair
jokes.explanation: Perfect character delusion. Dino's self-image vs reality creates ironic humor.

jokes.text: This weighs almost five lbs. You eat this? You put this in your body? - Yeah, it's good bread. It has no yeast in it. - It's not bread.
jokes.setup: Dino examining Larry's heavy bread
jokes.punchline: Dino's denial that unleavened brick is bread
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about health food absurdity. Dino's disbelief at Larry's diet.

jokes.text: By the way, I saw the show? It stinks. The sharks are swimming around. Nobody's even-- The sharks aren't eating anybody.
jokes.setup: Larry critiquing Dino's shark show
jokes.punchline: Complaint that sharks aren't violent enough
jokes.explanation: Larry's blunt TV criticism. Wants more violence from nature documentary.

jokes.text: And you know what? I'm not calling you Dog anymore. The Dog days are over. From now on, you're Dino!
jokes.setup: Larry's anger at Dino escalating
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'dog days' while revoking nickname privilege
jokes.explanation: Great pun and escalation. Larry weaponizing the nickname as punishment. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Take your fucking rabbit food and your goddamn five-lb bread and stick it up your fucking ass!
jokes.setup: Cabinet conflict reaching peak
jokes.punchline: Dino's explosive profanity contrasting with earlier 'peace' persona
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Dino's true nature. Fake hippie persona completely drops.

jokes.text: The sorry window? I didn't know there was a sorry window. - Two years, you're still getting sorries? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Heidi confused by Larry's condolence timing rules
jokes.punchline: Larry's arbitrary time limit on sympathy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry concept - 'sorry window' as social rule. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: That's like saying 'happy new year' in October.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining sorry window concept
jokes.punchline: Comparison to other time-sensitive greetings
jokes.explanation: Perfect analogy supporting the sorry window concept. Great logical extension.

jokes.text: Borrow, return.
jokes.setup: Heidi returning pen Larry doesn't need
jokes.punchline: Simple moral principle stated as universal law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - entire moral philosophy in two words. Sets up money conflict.

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sake. Is this about-- Is this about the 60-- The 60 bucks that you lent me? No, of course not! Although, I mean, now that you mention it. I did give you $60.
jokes.setup: Heidi realizing Larry's pen return is about money
jokes.punchline: Larry's transparent denial followed by immediate confession about the $60
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry can't help himself. His obvious lie immediately followed by the truth is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: I guess it's kind of, what? In your wallet now.
jokes.setup: Larry awkwardly bringing up the $60
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive way of asking for money back
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Larry's inability to directly ask for money back.

jokes.text: You've got something on your head. I believe they refer to that as a smiley face.
jokes.setup: Larry at funeral with sunblock smiley face burned into forehead
jokes.punchline: Formal observation of the inappropriate facial marking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual payoff to episode theme. Larry literally branded with thing he hates most.

jokes.text: They're frequently used by idiots at the end of emails and text messages, such as 'I miss you. :)'
jokes.setup: Explaining smiley faces at funeral
jokes.punchline: Calling users idiots while demonstrating inappropriate usage
jokes.explanation: Good callback and escalation. Funeral setting makes complaint more absurd.

jokes.text: Well, a disgruntled woman drew it with sunblock and then I fell asleep in the sun.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the smiley face on his forehead
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of bizarre revenge
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan explanation of absurd situation. Larry's casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How long do you think the... Grieving process might take for her?
jokes.setup: Larry at funeral asking about grieving
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately calculating mourning time for work purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry inappropriate focus on practical matters during tragedy.

jokes.text: You're shitting me? - Not shitting. - No shit? - No shit.
jokes.setup: Rosemary offers to work for Larry
jokes.punchline: Repeated 'shit' wordplay in different contexts
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay escalation. Not sophisticated but functional.

jokes.text: What an asshole he is.
jokes.setup: Rivkin learns Larry lied about wait time
jokes.punchline: Doctor's uncharacteristic profanity about patient
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing consequences of Larry's lie. Professional breaking character.

jokes.text: Yeah, sorry. Sorry about that. - What the hell's that sunburn on your head?
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for lying
jokes.punchline: Doctor distracted by absurd smiley face burn
jokes.explanation: Visual gag interrupting serious moment. Doctor's confusion over appearance.

jokes.text: If you don't sneak a peek at the penis, I'll-- I'll-- - I don't want to see that.
jokes.setup: Larry negotiating medical exam terms
jokes.punchline: Doctor's emphatic denial of interest in looking
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier medical anxiety. Doctor's professional frustration.

jokes.text: You're fucking Antoinette's mother? That's sick!
jokes.setup: Susie sees Larry with Rosemary at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Assumes sexual relationship based on limited information
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and misunderstanding. Susie's jump to wrong conclusion perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yes, I know, but I shat where I ate. And now I shall eat where I shat.
jokes.setup: Larry returning to restaurant after breakup
jokes.punchline: Completing the promised social experiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to episode-long setup. Larry following through on absurd social experiment with triumph.

jokes.text: Is everything okay with your meal? - Did you put something in the food? - No!
jokes.setup: Heidi checking on Larry's meal professionally
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid assumption about food tampering
jokes.explanation: Good ending escalation. Larry's paranoia about revenge through food shows social experiment has consequences.

tmdb_overview: Larry challenges the rules of dating and rethinks a decision regarding a new workplace neighbor. Meanwhile, Jeff invents an ill-advised excuse to get out of a dining engagement.
